briefing.headline: Rig up a Walking Topwater — steady spawn approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Walking Topwater is a strong choice for spawn with stained water and stable conditions.

Wheeler: Use monofilament (Sufix Siege) for topwater fishing — the stretch prevents jerking the bait away from the fish on the hookset. Pull into the fish rather than jerking, letting the sharp VMC hooks get into the fish

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Use monofilament (Sufix Siege) for topwater fishing — the stretch prevents jerking the bait away from the fish on the hookset. Pull into the fish rather than jerking, letting the sharp VMC ho
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, rock. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: In Florida heavy grass, switch to 30-lb braid with a 12-inch monofilament leader (Sufix Elite) for topwater — need to force fish out of hay-field grass or you'll lose them
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, rock. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: KVD: 'Don't assume you must finesse spawning bass. At the 2011 Classic, 4 of top 7 finishers power fished spinnerbaits and crankbaits on the same 150-yard spawning flat. Key conditions: hard bottom flat with stumps + grass, 10-degree water temp ri...
Palaniuk: 'Walk the frog on top of thick grass to get bass to blow through it, or fish it on edges and points of the grass. Walk it 90% of the time like a Zara Spook'
Robertson: Look for bass hunting bait in small feeding groups called 'wolf packs' — these aggressive groups will respond well to topwater presentations in pockets and around scattered rock.
Wheeler: When a bass dogs a prop bait without striking, pause it for as long as 6 seconds; the fish finally inhaled the topwater after the extended pause.

briefing.depthStrategy: Stable spawn conditions have fish committed to beds in 1-5ft. Focus on hard-bottom areas in protected coves, pockets, and flats. Polarized glasses are essential for sight-fishing.
adjustIf: If conditions suddenly shift (pressure drop, wind change), be ready to speed up your approach — transitional moments produce big bites
If water clears significantly, downsize presentations and move to natural color patterns
If fish are blowing up but not connecting, pause 3-5 seconds after each strike before resuming
